name: North Ossetia-Alania (Russia)

colors.symbolism: moral purity and the priestly caste
military valour and the warrior caste
prosperity, wealth and the farming/herding caste

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of white, red and yellow.
history: The flag was first adopted by South Ossetia in 1991 and subsequently used by the Republic of North Ossetia-Alania, which formally adopted it on 2 October 1994. The tricolour reflects the traditional Ossetian three-caste social division of clergy/sages, warriors and farmers.

features.symbolism: the three colours represent, from top to bottom, the moral purity (white), military valour (red) and prosperity (yellow) of the Ossetian people, drawing on the traditional caste division of Ossetian society.
